goal: Add CompactifAI (https://api.compactif.ai/v1) as a second LLM provider alongside Ollama. Users configure llm_provider = "compactifai" in config.toml (or pass --llm-provider compactifai at the CLI) to route LLM inference through the CompactifAI cloud API. The OpenAI-compatible Python client is used. API key management handles both CLI mode (stdin prompt → ~/.zshrc) and menu bar mode (macOS dialog → macOS Keychain). The openai Python package is installed automatically into the venv alongside ollama.

tasks.title: Add `provider` parameter and CompactifAI branch to `stream_response()` in `llm.py`
tasks.description:
Extend `stream_response()` in `src/core/llm.py` to accept a `provider` argument and route to the appropriate client.

Changes:
1. Add `import os` and `import openai` to the third-party imports block (alongside the existing `import ollama`). Import ordering: stdlib (`os`) before third-party (`ollama`, `openai`) per python-imports.mdc.
2. Add `provider: str = "ollama"` as the last parameter of `stream_response()`.
3. Wrap the existing ollama call in an `if provider == "ollama":` branch.
4. Add an `elif provider == "compactifai":` branch:
   - Read `api_key = os.environ.get("MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY")`.
   - If `api_key` is falsy, raise `RuntimeError("MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY environment variable not set")`.
   - Add `logger.debug("Calling CompactifAI", extra={"model": model_name})` (matches the existing ollama debug call pattern; structured fields per python-logging.mdc).
   - Build client: `openai.OpenAI(base_url="https://api.compactif.ai/v1", api_key=api_key)`.
   - Call `client.chat.completions.create(model=model_name, messages=messages, stream=True)`.
   - Yield `chunk.choices[0].delta.content or ""` for each chunk.
   - Catch `openai.OpenAIError` (not bare `Exception`) per python-errors.mdc ('catch the narrowest exception type possible'): `except openai.OpenAIError as err: raise RuntimeError(f"CompactifAI generation failed with model {model_name!r}") from err`.
5. Add an `else:` branch: `raise ValueError(f"Unknown provider: {provider!r}. Must be 'ollama' or 'compactifai'.")`.
6. Update the function docstring: add `provider` to Args, add `ValueError` for unknown provider to Raises.
7. Update the INTENT comment above `stream_response()` (python-ai-intent-comments.mdc: 'update the comment whenever the behaviour it describes changes'):
   - Purpose: now routes to ollama or CompactifAI based on `provider`.
   - Invariants: add `When provider == 'compactifai', MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY must be set in the environment.`
   - Safe to refactor: callers now also depend on the `provider` parameter contract.
8. Update the module-level docstring (first string in the file) to mention both providers: e.g. `'LLM entry point invoked as a subprocess by the Rust binary. Supports ollama (local) and CompactifAI (cloud) providers.'`
checks: `grep 'INTENT' src/core/llm.py` still present and updated to mention provider routing.

tasks.title: Add `--provider` CLI argument to `llm.py` `main()` and pass to `stream_response()`
tasks.description:
Update `main()` in `src/core/llm.py` to accept and forward the provider argument.

Changes:
1. Add to the `argparse` block:
   ```python
   parser.add_argument(
       "--provider", default="ollama",
       help="LLM provider: 'ollama' or 'compactifai' (default: ollama)"
   )
   ```
2. Update the `stream_response` call to pass `args.provider`:
   ```python
   for token in stream_response(transcript, args.prompt_file, args.model, args.provider):
   ```
checks: `python3 src/core/llm.py --help` includes `--provider` in the help output.

tasks.title: Add CompactifAI happy-path test to `llm_test.py`
tasks.description:
Add `test_stream_response_compactifai_yields_tokens()` to `src/core/llm_test.py`.

- Set `MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY` in the test environment via `os.environ`.
- Mock `openai.OpenAI` (patch `openai.OpenAI`) to return a fake streaming response: an iterable of objects where `chunk.choices[0].delta.content` is a non-empty string.
- Assert that `ollama.chat` is **not** called (patch it as well to guard against regression).
- Call `stream_response(transcript, prompt_path, "cai-llama-3-1-8b-slim", "compactifai")` and assert the yielded tokens are non-empty.
- Include a What/Why docstring following the pattern in `llm_test.py`.
- Clean up `MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY` from env after the test.

tasks.title: Add test: CompactifAI raises `RuntimeError` when `MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY` is absent
tasks.description:
Add `test_stream_response_compactifai_raises_on_missing_api_key()` to `src/core/llm_test.py`.

- Ensure `MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY` is not present in `os.environ` (pop it if it exists).
- Call `stream_response(transcript, prompt_path, "cai-llama-3-1-8b-slim", "compactifai")`.
- Assert `RuntimeError` is raised with a message containing `"MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY"`.
- Include a What/Why docstring.

tasks.title: Add test: CompactifAI raises `RuntimeError` when openai client raises
tasks.description:
Add `test_stream_response_compactifai_raises_on_api_failure()` to `src/core/llm_test.py`.

- Set `MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY` in `os.environ`.
- Mock `openai.OpenAI` so that calling `chat.completions.create(...)` raises a generic `Exception`.
- Assert `RuntimeError` is raised with a message containing `"CompactifAI generation failed"`.
- Include a What/Why docstring.
- Clean up env after the test.

tasks.title: Add test: unknown `provider` value raises `ValueError`
tasks.description:
Add `test_stream_response_raises_on_unknown_provider()` to `src/core/llm_test.py`.

- Call `stream_response(transcript, prompt_path, "llama3", "unknown_provider")`.
- Assert `ValueError` is raised with a message containing `"Unknown provider"`.
- Include a What/Why docstring.
checks: `python -m unittest src.core.llm_test` runs all 10 tests and passes.

tasks.title: Add `llm_provider` field to `AppConfig` and update `DEFAULT_CONFIG_TOML` in `src/config.rs`
tasks.description:
Two changes in `src/config.rs`:

1. Add the field to the `AppConfig` struct (after `llm_model`):
   ```rust
   pub llm_provider: String,
   ```
   And to the `Default` impl:
   ```rust
   llm_provider: "ollama".to_string(),
   ```

2. Update `DEFAULT_CONFIG_TOML` (the hardcoded string literal):
   - Change the comment header from `# whisper_model, llm_model: edit here and restart to apply` to `# whisper_model, llm_model, llm_provider: edit here and restart to apply`.
   - Update `llm_model` inline comment from `# Ollama model for LLM inference` to `# model name for the active LLM provider (see llm_provider)`.
   - Add the new line after `llm_model`:
     ```toml
     llm_provider = "ollama"    # LLM provider: "ollama" (local) or "compactifai" (cloud)
     ```
checks: Delete `~/.config/yaptap/config.toml`, launch app, open file — contains `llm_provider = "ollama"`.
`grep 'llm_provider' src/config.rs` returns matches in both the struct and the literal.

tasks.title: Add `provider` and `api_key` params to `run_llm_collect()` in `src/llm.rs`; pass `--provider` and inject env
tasks.description:
Extend `run_llm_collect()` in `src/llm.rs` to accept a provider string and an optional API key.

New signature:
```rust
pub fn run_llm_collect(
    transcript: &str,
    prompt_path: &Path,
    model: &str,
    provider: &str,
    api_key: Option<&str>,
    active_child: &Arc<Mutex<Option<Child>>>,
) -> Result<String>
```

In the command builder block, after `.args(["--model", model])`, add:
```rust
.args(["--provider", provider])
```

After the existing `.env("PATH", ...)` call, add:
```rust
if let Some(key) = api_key {
    cmd.env("MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY", key);
}
```

Add a `tracing::debug!` call with structured fields immediately before spawning (rust-tracing.mdc: 'Always log structured key-value fields'):
```rust
tracing::debug!(provider = %provider, has_api_key = api_key.is_some(), model = %model, "spawning llm.py");
```

Update the `/// # Contract` doc comment to document the new `provider` and `api_key` parameters.

All callers of `run_llm_collect` must be updated to pass the new arguments (see P13-T013 for `app.rs`).
checks: `grep 'provider' src/llm.rs` returns matches in the signature, command builder, and tracing call.

tasks.title: Add `--llm-provider` CLI flag to `src/main.rs` and pass `--provider` to `llm.py` inline spawn
tasks.description:
Changes in `src/main.rs`:

1. Add `llm_provider: Option<String>` to the `Args` struct alongside the existing `llm_model: Option<String>` field:
   ```rust
   /// Override the LLM provider: ollama or compactifai
   #[arg(long)]
   llm_provider: Option<String>,
   ```
   Validate accepted values: if the parsed value is not `"ollama"` or `"compactifai"`, print `error: unknown llm-provider '<value>' — must be 'ollama' or 'compactifai'` to stderr and exit 1.

2. Add `args.llm_provider.is_none()` to the mode-detection block (currently lines 138–147). The block currently checks `args.llm_model.is_none()` among others; `llm_provider` must follow the same pattern so that `--llm-provider` alone triggers CLI mode rather than launching the menu-bar app:
   ```rust
   && args.llm_provider.is_none()
   ```

3. Load config (already done for `llm_model`). Resolve effective provider with CLI flag overriding config:
   ```rust
   let provider = args.llm_provider
       .as_deref()
       .unwrap_or(&config.llm_provider);
   ```
   Add `tracing::debug!(provider = %provider, "resolved LLM provider")` immediately after (rust-tracing.mdc: structured fields).

4. In the inline `llm.py` spawn block (currently at lines 317–326), add `--provider` after `--model`:
   ```rust
   .args(["--model", llm_model])
   .args(["--provider", provider])
   ```

5. Update the `--llm-model` help text to `"Override the LLM model name for the active provider (default: llama3)"`.
checks: `yaptap --llm-provider unknown` prints the expected error and exits 1.
`grep '\-\-provider' src/main.rs` shows the arg is passed to the subprocess.
`grep 'resolved LLM provider' src/main.rs` returns a match.

tasks.title: Implement CLI API key prompt in `src/main.rs`
tasks.description:
Before the inline `llm.py` spawn in `src/main.rs`, when `provider == "compactifai"`:

1. Check `std::env::var("MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY")` — if `Ok(key)` and non-empty, add `tracing::debug!(source = "env", "MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY resolved")` and store `key`.
2. If missing or empty, prompt on stdout:
   ```
   CompactifAI API key not found.
   Enter your MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY: 
   ```
   Read from stdin (trim whitespace). If empty, print `error: API key must not be empty` to stderr and exit 1.
3. Append `\nexport MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY="<key>"\n` to `~/.zshrc` (create file if absent; append-open to preserve existing content). If the file write fails (permissions, etc.), log `tracing::warn!("failed to save API key to ~/.zshrc — key available for this session only")` and continue; do not exit.
4. Print to stdout: `Key saved to ~/.zshrc. Run 'source ~/.zshrc' or open a new terminal to apply globally.`
5. Inject key into the subprocess command via `.env("MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY", &key)` on the `Command` before spawning.

This path is only reached when `provider == "compactifai"` and the env var is absent.
checks: Unset `MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY`, run `yaptap --llm-provider compactifai --prompt email-reply` — CLI prompts for key, appends to `~/.zshrc`, proceeds to spawn.
`grep MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY ~/.zshrc` shows the export line after the above.
With `MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY` set in env, the prompt does not appear.
`grep 'source = ' src/main.rs` shows the structured tracing call.

tasks.title: Add `security-framework` crate to `Cargo.toml`
tasks.description:
Add the `security-framework` crate for macOS Keychain access (used by P13-T012).

In `Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]`:
```toml
security-framework = "2"
```

This crate provides `SecKeychainItem`, `SecKeychainAddGenericPassword`, `SecKeychainFindGenericPassword`, etc., for reading and writing Keychain items.

tasks.title: Add Keychain read/write helpers for `MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY` (new module `src/keychain.rs`)
checks: `cargo test` passes (the round-trip test is `#[ignore]` so it does not block CI).
`grep 'pub(crate)' src/keychain.rs` returns matches for both functions.
`grep '//!' src/keychain.rs` returns the module doc comment.
`grep '# Errors' src/keychain.rs` returns a match on `keychain_set_api_key`.

tasks.title: Menu bar API key resolution flow and updated `run_llm_collect()` call in `src/app.rs`
tasks.description:
In `src/app.rs`, make two changes to `run_pipeline_inner`:

**A. Condition the `ollama_available()` check on provider (line ~619)**

The existing check at line 619 runs for ALL prompts:
```rust
if !ollama_available() { anyhow::bail!(...) }
```
Wrap it so it only fires for the Ollama provider:
```rust
if config.llm_provider == "ollama" && !ollama_available() {
    anyhow::bail!(
        "Ollama not running. Start Ollama and try again.\n\
         Run `ollama serve` in a terminal, or open the Ollama app."
    );
}
```
For `"compactifai"` this check is skipped entirely.

**B. Add provider-aware API key resolution before the `run_llm_collect()` call (line ~627)**

1. Read effective provider from `config.llm_provider`.
2. If provider is `"compactifai"`:
   a. Check `std::env::var("MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY")` — if set and non-empty, store as `api_key: Option<String>`.
   b. Else check `crate::keychain::keychain_get_api_key()` — if `Some(key)`, use it.
   c. Else show an API key input dialog. The existing `show_alert()` helper (line ~138) only supports button choices with no text field, so this dialog must be built with a raw `objc` NSAlert+NSTextField, following the same `msg_send!` pattern already used in `app.rs`:
      - Allocate and configure an `NSAlert`.
      - Set title: `"CompactifAI API Key Required"`.
      - Set message: `"Enter your MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY to continue."`.
      - Add accessory view: an `NSSecureTextField` (so the key is masked).
      - Add buttons: **Save** (tag 1000) and **Cancel** (tag 1001).
      - Run the alert modally; read the text-field string value on **Save**.
   d. On **Cancel** (or empty input on Save): show a standard `show_alert()` message (`"CompactifAI provider requires an API key. Recording cancelled."`), transition state machine back to Idle, return early.
   e. On **Save** with non-empty input: call `crate::keychain::keychain_set_api_key(&input)`, store as `api_key`.
3. For `"ollama"`: `api_key` is `None`.
4. Update the `run_llm_collect()` call to pass the new `provider` and `api_key` arguments:
   ```rust
   crate::llm::run_llm_collect(
       &transcript,
       &prompt_path,
       &config.llm_model,
       &config.llm_provider,
       api_key.as_deref(),
       &shared.child,
   )
   ```
checks: With `compactifai` provider and key in Keychain: hotkey → speak → result on clipboard (no dialog shown).
With `compactifai` provider and no key anywhere: dialog appears; Cancel returns to Idle; Save stores in Keychain and proceeds.
With `ollama` provider: no key resolution logic runs, existing behaviour unchanged.

tasks.title: Add `openai` to pip install args in `run_setup_commands()` in `src/app.rs`
tasks.description:
In `src/app.rs`, update the pip install command (currently at line ~219) to include `openai`:

Current:
```rust
.args(["install", "--quiet", "numpy<2", "openai-whisper", "ollama", "tomli"])
```

Replace with:
```rust
.args(["install", "--quiet", "numpy<2", "openai-whisper", "ollama", "openai", "tomli"])
```
checks: Remove `~/.config/yaptap/.venv/`, trigger first-launch setup, confirm `pip show openai` in the new venv shows a version.

tasks.title: Update `venv_healthy()` in `src/app.rs` to import-check `openai`
tasks.description:
In `venv_healthy()` (currently at line ~232 in `src/app.rs`), extend the Python health-check expression to also import `openai`.

Current check expression (Python inline string):
```python
import numpy; v=numpy.__version__.split('.'); assert int(v[0]) < 2, 'numpy>=2'; import sys; m='tomllib' if sys.version_info>=(3,11) else 'tomli'; __import__(m)
```

Extend to append `; import openai` at the end of the expression string.

tasks.title: Update `try_pip_repair()` in `src/app.rs` to include `openai`
tasks.description:
In `try_pip_repair()` (currently at line ~257 in `src/app.rs`), update the pip install args to include `openai`.

Current:
```rust
.args(["install", "--quiet", "numpy<2", "tomli"])
```

Replace with:
```rust
.args(["install", "--quiet", "numpy<2", "openai-whisper", "ollama", "openai", "tomli"])
```

Installing the full package list (rather than just the historically missing ones) ensures repair is idempotent and covers any missing package, not just those known at the time of writing.
checks: With `openai` absent from venv: launch app — `try_pip_repair()` runs, `pip show openai` shows a version after launch.

tasks.title: Validate `llm_provider` value in `AppConfig::load()` in `src/config.rs`
checks: Set `llm_provider = "badvalue"` in `~/.config/yaptap/config.toml`, launch app — alert appears with the expected message and `config.llm_provider` is `"ollama"` in memory.
